findings.message: Consumer pact test assertion is incompatible with the new get_stock() return type. The test asserts get_stock() == 5 (int), but the function now returns dict[str, int]. This weakens the consumer contract and breaks the contract-testable guarantee.
findings.suggestion: Update the assertion to verify both fields: assert get_stock(str(srv.url), 1) == {"in_stock": 5, "reserved_count": ...} and update the pact interaction to include the reserved_count field with an appropriate matcher.

findings.message: Pact interaction does not declare reserved_count field that the provider is now expected to return. The client code at src/demo/clients/inventory.py:14 requires reserved_count from the response, but the pact mock only provides item_id and in_stock. The pact interaction must be regenerated to include this field.
findings.suggestion: Update the pact interaction to include reserved_count: .with_body({"item_id": match.int(1), "in_stock": match.int(5), "reserved_count": match.int(...)})

findings.message: Client code comment acknowledges reserved_count is not declared in the pact interaction, indicating a known but unresolved contract mismatch. This violates the contract-driven testing principle and will cause provider verification to fail.
findings.suggestion: Do not access fields (reserved_count) that are not explicitly declared in the pact interaction. Either update the pact interaction first, or remove the reserved_count usage until the pact is updated.
